warnings.title: A non-primitive object is passed as a route argument
warnings.why: Flutter passes a live Dart object through `Navigator.pushNamed(arguments:)`. A URL route can carry an id, not an object graph.
warnings.impact: The destination screen must re-fetch or re-derive the object from an identifier, which changes its data-loading shape.
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: Lower the argument to a route parameter (an id) plus a data load on the destination route; flag when the object cannot be reduced to an identifier.

warnings.title: Module-level mutable store singleton
warnings.why: A top-level `ChangeNotifier` instance is process-global. In Flutter that is one user. On a Next.js server the module is shared across *all* requests.
warnings.impact: If such a store is touched during SSR, one user can see another user's cart. This is a correctness and privacy bug, not a styling one.
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: Emit the store as a per-request/provider-scoped instance and mark the component tree that touches it as client-only.

warnings.title: Platform channels
warnings.why: MethodChannel/EventChannel call into native Android/iOS code. There is no native side on the web.
warnings.impact: Any feature behind the channel cannot be converted at all; it needs a web implementation written by a human (an HTTP endpoint, a browser API, or a stub).
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: Emit an Opaque node and require an override; never guess a substitute.

warnings.title: Isolates / compute()
warnings.why: Isolates are Dart's concurrency model. The browser equivalent (Web Workers) has a different memory and messaging model.
warnings.impact: Work offloaded from the UI thread would run on the main thread instead, or must be rewritten against Web Workers.
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: Out of scope for the MVP: flag as unsupported and require an override.

warnings.title: CustomPainter / CustomPaint
warnings.why: Imperative canvas drawing. Spec §7 names this as the automation boundary.
warnings.impact: The painted widget cannot be generated; it needs a hand-written React component.
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: Emit an Opaque node keyed by a stable anchor and route it to the override system, so the human writes it once and regeneration preserves it.

warnings.title: Explicit animation (AnimationController / TickerProvider)
warnings.why: Explicit animation choreography is outside the MVP; only implicit animations are planned to map cleanly.
warnings.impact: Transitions driven by a controller will not be reproduced.
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: The animation runtime handles the implicit cases; a controller timeline flags for an override.

warnings.title: Web-incompatible import: dart:isolate
warnings.why: Isolates have no direct React analogue.
warnings.impact: Any code path reaching this import cannot run in a browser, converted or not.
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: Refuse to convert the enclosing unit; require an override or a web-specific implementation.

warnings.title: Web-incompatible import: dart:ffi
warnings.why: dart:ffi is native interop; there is no web or React analogue.
warnings.impact: Any code path reaching this import cannot run in a browser, converted or not.
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: Refuse to convert the enclosing unit; require an override or a web-specific implementation.

warnings.title: Web-incompatible import: dart:io
warnings.why: dart:io is not available on the web at all.
warnings.impact: Any code path reaching this import cannot run in a browser, converted or not.
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: Refuse to convert the enclosing unit; require an override or a web-specific implementation.

warnings.title: StreamBuilder
warnings.why: Streams are outside the frozen MVP (only FutureBuilder is in).
warnings.impact: Live-updating UI will not be generated at MVP.
warnings.likelyCompilerBehavior: Planned: map to a subscription (useSyncExternalStore / query subscription). Recognised, not yet supported.

compatibility.reasoning: The application depends on capabilities that do not exist in a browser at all — web-incompatible imports (dart:isolate, dart:ffi, dart:io), platform channels and isolates. These are not gaps in the compiler; they are gaps in the target platform. Converting this application means a human first decides what those capabilities become on the web. The UI layer itself may still convert well — see the supported/partial lists.
